plan_summary.primary_goal: Establish a financially resilient community clay workshop in Nuuk on a 2M DKK Year 1 budget, hitting a 40/35/25 revenue mix while preserving a 15% contingency.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Year 1 break-even and contingency survival: tests whether the speculative drop-in/rental revenue stream clears its 25% target at the minimum viable hourly rate, and whether the 15% contingency absorbs labor-law reclassification and rental-shortfall shocks.

key_values.label: Year 1 total budget
key_values.comment: Total allocated capital envelope for Year 1; every other DKK quantity is benchmarked against this.

key_values.label: Contingency reserve share of budget
key_values.comment: Ring-fenced share of Year 1 budget reserved as untouchable contingency against labor, rental, and utility shocks.
key_values.source_text: Contingency reserve commitment: 15% of total Year 1 budget

key_values.label: Drop-in/rental share of Year 1 revenue
key_values.comment: Speculative share of revenue from off-peak hourly rentals; flagged as the most fragile revenue stream.
key_values.source_text: Year 1 revenue mix estimate: 25% from drop-ins/rentals

key_values.label: Minimum viable hourly rental rate
key_values.comment: Minimum hourly rental rate that covers off-peak overhead; binding floor for the speculative drop-in revenue stream.
key_values.source_text: Minimum viable hourly rental rate target of 165 DKK/hour

key_values.label: FTE equivalent staffing
key_values.comment: Four part-time instructors equate to two FTE salary equivalents driving the annual labor cost line.
key_values.source_text: Staffing model equivalence: two (2) FTE salary equivalents

key_values.label: Labor-law non-compliance cost shock
key_values.comment: Reclassification to salaried structure inflates fixed labor cost by roughly 20-35%; midpoint is the base shock factor.
key_values.source_text: fixed labor cost increases by 20-35% in Year 1

derived_questions.question: After paying the baseline annual labor cost, does the 2M DKK budget still leave a positive margin for fit-out, materials, and utilities?
derived_questions.why_it_matters: Identifies whether the largest fixed expense (instructor pay) leaves operating room within the Year 1 envelope before contingency is even touched.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Year 1 total revenue target
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: The revenue-mix shares are fractions of total revenue, not of budget; without it no revenue-stream coverage formula can resolve.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Anchor near the 2M DKK budget envelope and stress-test ±30%; refine once annual operating cost is modelled.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Monthly cost per FTE equivalent
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives the annual labor cost line; the entire labor-law shock and budget-after-labor margin depend on it.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Use Greenlandic skilled-instructor pay benchmarks (roughly 30k-45k DKK/month) and bound around the contractor midpoint.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Operational months in Year 1
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Annualises both labor cost and revenue against the 2M DKK envelope; the digest flags this as a missing primitive.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Anchor at 12 months with downside 9 (delayed September 2026 readiness) and upside 12.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Available off-peak rental hours per year
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Caps the bottom-up drop-in revenue (rate * hours) and tests whether the 25% mix share is mechanically reachable.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Estimate weekly off-peak slots times operating weeks; bound 600-1800 hours/year.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Off-peak rental utilization fraction
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Without an uptake rate the off-peak hours cap converts to revenue at 100%, which the source explicitly flags as unrealistic.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Bound 0.2-0.6 around a base near 0.4; aligned with the source's <60% deficit trigger.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Contingency reserve in DKK
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Anchors every downstream shock-absorption test against a single named pool.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Annual labor cost (baseline contractor model)
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Largest fixed expense line and the base on which the labor-law shock multiplies.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Drop-in revenue shortfall vs. target
recommended_first_calculations.formula_hint: drop_in_revenue_shortfall_dkk = max(0, year1_revenue_target_dkk * drop_in_revenue_mix_share - minimum_viable_hourly_rate_dkk * off_peak_hours_per_year * drop_in_utilization_rate)
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Quantifies the named risk that speculative off-peak rentals fall short and debit the contingency.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Labor-law reclassification shock magnitude
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Converts the 20-35% shock fraction into an absolute DKK pressure debited from the contingency reserve.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Combined contingency surplus after shocks
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Top-level viability gate: the single 15% reserve must absorb both named shocks from the same pool.

unmodelled_gates.label: Greenlandic labor-law contractor classification
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Plan budgets assume contractor classification; a non-compliance ruling forces mandatory salaried structure and could deplete the 2M DKK budget independently of any operational threshold.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Mandatory severance/salary cost overrides the 15% contingency and may consume the entire Year 1 budget.

unmodelled_gates.label: Nuuk municipal utility variance for high-draw kiln
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Centralized kiln operation depends on a zoning/utility variance the plan treats as a fixed pre-condition; without it the workshop cannot fire ceramics.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Fit-out cannot complete, Q3 readiness target missed, and no kiln-based revenue is generated.

unmodelled_gates.label: Commercial lease near Katuaq by 2026-07-01
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: All downstream timelines and material-buffer prepayments are conditional on securing the lease on time; treated as binary in the source narrative.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Material buffer prepayment contracts cannot proceed and the September 2026 readiness target is lost.
